meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/entra) — admin-center paths and setting labels grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03. Shares mechanisms with the Tier1 identity-directory guidance; Tier0 target values applied.
generalNotes: Device settings (device quota, user registration, LAPS toggle, BitLocker self-recovery) are in Entra ID > Devices > Device settings. User default-permission toggles are in Entra ID > Users > User settings. Guest/external settings are in Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings.
Most of these are Global Administrator changes; the BitLocker self-recovery restriction specifically needs at least Privileged Role Administrator.
These are the same underlying settings as the Tier1 identity/device rows — values here reflect the Tier0 baseline (e.g. device limit 10 rather than the Tier1 default 20).

requiredSettings.label: Maximum number of devices per user
requiredSettings.value: 10 (Tier0 baseline — more restrictive than the Tier1 default of 20; both joined and registered devices count against it)

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Device settings.
Set 'Maximum number of devices per user' = 10 (or 'Unlimited' only if intentionally uncapped).
Save.
Verify: the value reads 10. Note (LOW impact): invisible to most users — only affects anyone who registers/joins more than 10 devices; confirm 10 is a deliberate choice vs the Tier1 default of 20.

requiredSettings.label: Users may register their devices with Microsoft Entra
requiredSettings.value: All (permissive default — required for Intune/MDM enrollment and BYOD registration)

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Device settings.
Confirm 'Users may register their devices with Microsoft Entra' = All (this is the default; the Tier0 row uses the default, not an override).
Save if changed.

Verify: the setting reads All. Note (LOW impact): this is an ENABLER, not a restriction — leave it at All unless a stricter posture (e.g. only Intune-enrolled corporate devices) is explicitly required, in which case restrict registration and pair with a compliant-device CA policy.

requiredSettings.label: Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)
Also required
requiredSettings.value: A client-side LAPS policy deployed via Intune (Endpoint security > Account protection) with BackupDirectory = Microsoft Entra ID — the tenant toggle alone rotates nothing

requiredSettings.label: Scope / prerequisites
requiredSettings.value: Entra joined or hybrid-joined Windows devices only (not Entra-registered); needs the April 2023+ Windows update. Passwords are readable only by Cloud Device Admin / Intune Admin / Helpdesk Admin roles.

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Device settings.
Set 'Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)' = Yes and Save (needs at least Cloud Device Administrator).
Deploy the companion client-side LAPS policy in Intune (Endpoint security > Account protection) with the backup directory set to Microsoft Entra ID — this defines rotation schedule and account.
Verify: retrieve a password via Entra ID > Devices > Local administrator password recovery for an enrolled device.
Note (LOW impact): invisible to end users — support staff pull a unique per-device local admin password from Entra instead of a shared secret.

requiredSettings.label: Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to
requiredSettings.value: Yes (admin consent workflow enabled — IsEnabled = True)

requiredSettings.label: Reviewers
requiredSettings.value: 3 designated reviewers who receive and act on requests (verify they are current and actively monitoring)

requiredSettings.label: Notifications / reminders / expiry
requiredSettings.value: Email notifications = Enabled, reminders = Enabled, request expiry = 30 days

steps: Entra ID > Enterprise applications > Consent and permissions > Admin consent settings.
Set 'Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to' = Yes.
Add the 3 reviewers, enable email notifications and reminders, and set request expiry = 30 days.
Save (can take up to an hour to take effect).

Verify: a standard user hitting an app that needs admin consent sees a 'request approval' flow (not a hard block), and reviewers get an email. Note (MEDIUM impact): users experience a delay pending review — keep the reviewer list current so requests don't stall. Pairs with the user-consent settings that route them here.

requiredSettings.label: Allow external users to remove themselves from your organization

steps: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings.
Ensure 'Allow external users to remove themselves from your organization' = Yes.
Save.

Verify: reads Yes. Note (LOW impact): a privacy/compliance convenience — guests can self-remove via the My Account portal; no effect on internal users and no security risk (a guest leaving only revokes their own access).

requiredSettings.label: Guest user access
requiredSettings.value: Restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive — Restricted Guest User role 10dae51f-b6af-4016-8d66-8c2a99b929b3)

requiredSettings.label: Guest invite settings (AllowInvitesFrom)
requiredSettings.value: adminsAndGuestInviters — only admins and users with the Guest Inviter role can invite guests

steps: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings.
Set Guest user access = 'Guest user access is restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive)'.
Set Guest invite settings = 'Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users' (admins + Guest Inviter role).
Save (guest changes can take up to ~15 minutes to propagate).

Verify: a standard (non-inviter) user can't invite external guests to Teams/SharePoint; existing guests can only see their own directory object. Note (MEDIUM impact): teams that collaborate externally must route invitations through IT / a Guest Inviter — set expectations and grant the Guest Inviter role where warranted. Pairs with the guest CA policies.

requiredSettings.label: Users can register applications
requiredSettings.value: No (AllowedToCreateApps = False — grant back via the Application Developer role as needed)

requiredSettings.label: Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants
Restrict users from recovering BitLocker key(s) for their owned devices
requiredSettings.value: Yes (AllowedToReadBitLockerKeysForOwnedDevice = False) — set in Devices > Device settings, needs Privileged Role Administrator

requiredSettings.label: Read other users
requiredSettings.value: Left enabled (AllowedToReadOtherUsers = True, default) — Graph/PowerShell only; don't disable (breaks Teams and other services)

steps: Entra ID > Users > User settings > Default user role permissions: set 'Users can register applications' = No, 'Users can create security groups' = No, and 'Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants' = Yes. Save.

The BitLocker restriction is NOT on this page: go to Entra ID > Devices > Device settings and set 'Restrict non-admin users from recovering the BitLocker key(s) for their owned devices' = Yes (requires at least Privileged Role Administrator).
Leave 'Read other users' at its default (enabled) — it's Graph-only and disabling it breaks directory reads in Teams and elsewhere.
Verify: a standard user no longer sees options to register an app or create a security group; a self-service BitLocker key lookup now sends them to the helpdesk.

Note (MEDIUM impact): power users who self-registered apps or created groups now need an explicit role (Application Developer / Groups roles) — assign those to specific people rather than reverting the tenant-wide setting.
